title: andropogon sorghum subsp. effusus
intro: Andropogon sorghum subsp. effusus, also known as Johnson grass, is a perennial grass species native to Africa and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Johnson grass are attributed to its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes. Johnson grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for various health conditions.
